title: Move acme-widgets dolt to server mode to unblock concurrent writes (and bd doctor)
description: The acme-widgets beads database is currently in embedded mode ('dolt_mode': 'embedded' in .beads/metadata.json). Embedded mode is single-writer: when mayor + tech-lead + reviewer run bd commands concurrently, we hit 'another process holds the exclusive lock on .beads/embeddeddolt' errors and have to retry. We've worked around this with shell retry loops, but it's friction. Server mode (the gc-default for the SFI rig itself) supports concurrent writers cleanly. Bonus: 'bd doctor' is not yet supported in embedded mode — moving to server unblocks it, which may itself be enough to diagnose and repair the wisps/wisp_dependencies schema bug (see aw-882). Plan: stop active bd users, edit .beads/metadata.json to set 'dolt_mode': 'server', start dolt server (bd dolt start auto-starts), verify db connection, then run bd doctor to see if it can repair the missing tables. Acceptance: two parallel bd write commands from different shells both succeed; bd doctor runs without 'not supported in embedded mode' error.
notes:
REVIEW PASSED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30):

Verified at 95d5503:
- .beads/metadata.json: dolt_mode=server ✓ (database=acme_widgets_private)
- bd dolt show: Server connection OK on port 56617 ✓
- bd doctor: runs cleanly (66 passed, 6 unrelated warnings — CLI version, role, git hooks, working tree, upstream-ahead, plugin version); no 'not yet supported in embedded mode' error ✓
- Two parallel bd update writes targeting different beads (aw-2k9 + aw-882) from separate background processes both exit 0 ✓; no embedded-lock errors

Re tech-lead's data-dir question: copying embeddeddolt → dolt is acceptable for now. The .beads/dolt/ tree is gitignored, so the only thing in the commit is the metadata.json flip — clean. Leaving the embedded copy as a fallback is fine; can be reaped later when confidence is high. Not blocking.

Closing.
close_reason: Peer review passed at 95d5503. Server mode active, bd doctor unblocked, parallel cross-bead writes succeed.

title: Repair beads schema: missing wisps and wisp_dependencies tables (breaks mail/sling)
description: The acme-widgets beads database is missing the 'wisps' and 'wisp_dependencies' tables. This causes 'gc mail send' (mail = wisp beads) and 'bd delete' (uses wisp_dependencies) to fail with 'Error 1146: table not found'. The bug is non-blocking for the implement-review loop because bead state (close + push) is the authoritative accept signal — mail is decorative. The likely cause is that the database was bootstrapped from a remote with an older schema, before the wisps tables were introduced. Fix: run 'bd migrate' to apply pending schema migrations. Do NOT use 'bd init --force' — that destroys the local database. Verify after: 'gc mail send mayor --subject test' should succeed; 'bd delete <some-spurious-id> --force' should not error on wisp_dependencies. Switching dolt_mode (embedded vs server) does NOT fix this — the schema is the same regardless of how it's accessed.
notes:
REVIEW PASSED — NO-OP CLOSE APPROVED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30):

Independently re-ran the four probes from a fresh shell:
- gc mail send mayor → sent aw-wisp-b9f ✓ (wisps table healthy)
- bd delete aw-nonexistent-zzz --force → clean 'issue not found' (no wisp_dependencies error) ✓
- bd dep add aw-882 aw-2k9 → succeeded (wisp_dependencies write) ✓
- bd dep remove aw-882 aw-2k9 → succeeded (wisp_dependencies delete) ✓

Re tech-lead's question (close with zero commits OK?): YES. The bead's acceptance criteria are functional ('gc mail send should succeed', 'bd delete should not error on wisp_dependencies') — not commit-count based. The actual fix was the data-dir swing in aw-2k9 (95d5503), already pushed. Resolving aw-882 as NO-OP with notes-only is correct; nothing to commit, nothing to push.

Closing.
close_reason: NO-OP. Schema was healthy after aw-2k9 made server mode point at the correct database; functional probes (gc mail send, bd delete, bd dep add/rm) all clean. Zero-commit close per bead intent.

title: Force Bun runtime for vite scripts so old system Node doesn't break dev/build
description: When 'bun run dev' is invoked, Bun resolves the 'vite' bin which has a '#!/usr/bin/env node' shebang, so it runs under whatever Node is on PATH. Trainees with an older system Node (<15) hit syntax errors on modern JS like '||='. The fix is to use the '--bun' flag in package.json scripts so vite runs on Bun's runtime. Update scripts to: 'dev': 'bun --bun vite', 'build': 'tsc -b && bun --bun vite build', 'preview': 'bun --bun vite preview'. Acceptance: a fresh shell with Node 14 or older on PATH should still run all package.json scripts correctly via bun.
notes:
REVIEW PASSED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30):

Tested with /tmp/badnode/node (exits 99 with FAILURE log) prepended to PATH; system Node moved out:
- bun run typecheck: exit 0
- bun run build: exit 0, dist/ + hashed JS produced
- bun run dev: Vite ready on :5173, HTTP 200 with #root + main.tsx
- bun run preview: served dist on :4173, HTTP 200
The stub was never invoked (no FAILURE line in any output). Acceptance criterion satisfied.

Re tech-lead's scope question: extending 'bun --bun' to tsc and tsc -b is CORRECT and necessary. The bead description's exact strings only listed vite invocations, but tsc has the same #!/usr/bin/env node fallthrough. Without wrapping tsc, both typecheck and build's first step would have hit old/bad system Node and failed the acceptance criterion ('all package.json scripts'). The description's strings were inconsistent with the acceptance criterion; the criterion wins. Approved as-is.

Closing.
close_reason: Retroactive peer review passed at c056e0f. Stubbed-node verification: typecheck/build/dev/preview all pass; stub never invoked. Tech-lead's scope expansion to tsc approved (necessary to satisfy 'all package.json scripts' acceptance criterion).

# Move ADR + sync docs/current — design

## Move

```bash
git mv docs/future/0001.ADR.TECH_STACK.md docs/decision-records/0001.ADR.TECH_STACK.md
```

`git mv` makes the rename obvious in review; `mv + git add` would also work since git tracks renames by content.

## Update ADR header

- Flip `Status: Proposed` → `Status: Accepted`. Per `DOCUMENTATION.md` lifecycle step 2, this is the implementation branch — Accepted is correct here.
- Resolve the ADR's "Open questions": runtime CSV fetch was chosen by .6. Either delete the open question or annotate "Resolved: runtime fetch (PR #X)".
- Do not retroactively add new sections — keep the historical record clean.

## Sync `docs/current/`

If any earlier task deviated from ADR 0001, reflect it here. Specifically:
- **Tailwind version** chosen in .2 (v4 vs v3) — note in a new `docs/current/STACK.md` listing as-built versions.
- **GitHub Pages SPA fallback** trick (`404.html` postbuild copy) — document in `docs/current/` so the next maintainer doesn't strip it.
- **`BASE_URL` convention** for asset/data fetches and router basename — document so future code follows the same pattern.

## Cross-link audit

`docs/current/INFORMATION_ARCHITECTURE.md` already references `../decision-records/0001.ADR.TECH_STACK.md`. After the move, that link resolves — verify by clicking through in a Markdown preview.

## Roadmap symlink discrepancy

`DOCUMENTATION.md` says `roadmap → future` is a symlink. Currently `docs/roadmap/` is a real directory with a duplicate ADR. Either fix in this task (replace with symlink: `rm -rf docs/roadmap && ln -s future docs/roadmap`) or file a follow-up bead — document the choice in the PR.

## README

Add a Quick Start section: clone, `bun install`, `bun run dev`, `bun run test`, `bun run test:e2e`, link to the live Pages URL.

## Constraints

- This is the LAST task in the epic. Do not start until .10 and .11 are merged.
- After this PR merges to `main`, the ADR in `decision-records/` is **frozen** per `DOCUMENTATION.md`. New decisions get new ADRs.

title: GitHub Pages deploy workflow on merge to main
description: Per ADR 0001: deploy the static Vite build output to GitHub Pages on merge to main. Configure base path in vite.config if needed for project pages.
design:
# GitHub Pages deploy — design

## ⚠️ BLOCKER — verify repo visibility

Repo is `bryanhirsch/acme-widgets-private`. The `-private` suffix and current `git remote` strongly suggest the repo is **private**. **GitHub Pages free tier requires a PUBLIC repo.** Private-repo Pages requires GitHub Pro/Team/Enterprise. This must be resolved before .11 ships — see the human-decision bead filed alongside this review. Options:
1. Make the repo public (default recommendation; this is a training example).
2. Accept GitHub Pro cost.
3. Switch host (Cloudflare Pages, Netlify) and amend ADR 0001.

## Vite base path

In `vite.config.ts`:
```ts
base: process.env.VITE_BASE ?? "/"
```

Build step in CI sets `VITE_BASE=/acme-widgets-private/` because this is a project page (`bryanhirsch.github.io/acme-widgets-private/`), not a user page. Local dev keeps the default `/`.

## Router basename

Already specified by .3: `createBrowserRouter(routes, { basename: import.meta.env.BASE_URL })`. Verify .3 is implemented this way before .11 lands. Do not duplicate the prefix in `<Link to>` calls.

## SPA fallback (CRITICAL)

GitHub Pages serves `404.html` for unknown paths. Without a fallback, refreshing on `/widgets` 404s. Fix: copy `dist/index.html` → `dist/404.html` as a postbuild step.

`package.json`:
```json
"postbuild": "cp -f dist/index.html dist/404.html"
```

(Use `cp -f` per AGENTS.md non-interactive rule.) Document this trick in `docs/current/` (.12).

## Workflow — `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

```yaml
on:
  push: { branches: [main] }
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false   # queue, don't cancel in-flight deploys
```

Two jobs:
1. `build`: checkout, setup-bun, `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `VITE_BASE=/acme-widgets-private/ bun run build`, `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3` with `path: dist`.
2. `deploy`: `needs: build`, `environment: { name: github-pages, url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }} }`, `actions/deploy-pages@v4`.

## Repo settings (manual, document in PR)

Settings → Pages → Source = **GitHub Actions** (NOT "Deploy from a branch"). The workflow alone cannot set this.

## Constraints

- Do NOT couple deploy to CI (`workflow_run`). Couple deploy to push-to-main, and rely on branch protection (.10) to ensure main is only ever advanced by green PRs.
- Do NOT serve `dist/` from a branch — it's gitignored from .1 and stays gitignored.
acceptance_criteria: Push to main triggers the deploy workflow; the deployment URL appears in the workflow run summary. https://bryanhirsch.github.io/acme-widgets-private/ loads the home page. Direct deep-link to /acme-widgets-private/widgets and /acme-widgets-private/orders both load (404.html SPA fallback works). All asset URLs in the loaded page are prefixed with /acme-widgets-private/. The Pages source in repo settings is set to "GitHub Actions".
notes:
REVIEW PASSED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30):

Verified at 645f4ec.

The bead's literal ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA still references /acme-widgets-private/ paths, but the ARCHITECTURE override (deploys from public remote bryanhirsch/acme-widgets, path prefix /acme-widgets/) plus the .13 close resolution corrects this. Implementation correctly uses /acme-widgets/. Reading criteria with that substitution.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (interpreted per ARCHITECTURE override):
1. Push to main triggers deploy + URL in workflow summary — VERIFIED BY YAML: trigger push:branches:[main]; deploy job environment.url uses steps.deployment.outputs.page_url. Live verification on first push to main on the public remote.
2. https://bryanhirsch.github.io/acme-widgets/ loads home — POST-DEPLOY. Pre-merge evidence: VITE_BASE=/acme-widgets/ build emits dist/index.html with correct prefixes; SPA boots Home (.5) via index entry.
3. Deep-link /acme-widgets/widgets and /acme-widgets/orders work — VERIFIED BY ARTIFACTS: postbuild creates dist/404.html byte-identical to dist/index.html; GH Pages serves 404.html on unknown subpath; SPA boots and createBrowserRouter basename=BASE_URL=/acme-widgets/ routes correctly.
4. All asset URLs prefixed /acme-widgets/ — VERIFIED LOCALLY: index.html refs '/acme-widgets/assets/index-*.js' and '/acme-widgets/assets/index-*.css'; bundle inlines '/acme-widgets/data/widgets.csv'.
5. Pages source = 'GitHub Actions' — MANUAL repo Settings → Pages action on bryanhirsch/acme-widgets; tech-lead correctly surfaced in commit/notes for PR description.

Static structural review (23/23 PASS):
- Triggers: push→main + workflow_dispatch ✓; NOT coupled to CI via workflow_run ✓ (DESIGN constraint)
- Permissions: contents: read, pages: write, id-token: write ✓
- Concurrency: group=pages, cancel-in-progress=false (queue, don't cancel in-flight) ✓
- Two jobs: build + deploy(needs:build) ✓
- Build: checkout@v4 → setup-bun@v2 pinned 1.3.11 (no 'latest') → install --frozen-lockfile → 'bun run build' with VITE_BASE=/acme-widgets/ → upload-pages-artifact@v3 path=dist ✓
- Deploy: environment.name=github-pages, environment.url=page_url, deploy-pages@v4, id: deployment for outputs ✓

Build artifact verification:
- Default build (no VITE_BASE): assets at /assets/*; preserves dev/preview/e2e ✓
- Pages build (VITE_BASE=/acme-widgets/): assets at /acme-widgets/assets/*, CSV at /acme-widgets/data/widgets.csv inlined in bundle ✓
- postbuild creates dist/404.html byte-identical to dist/index.html (SPA fallback ready) ✓
- Regression check: typecheck=0, default-base build=0, e2e 3/3 pass ✓

REVIEWER NOTE: criterion #2 (live URL loads) is observed on the first push to main. If broken, file a new bug — do not reopen .11. Tech-lead's NOTES already acknowledge this iteration model.

Closing.

Workflow structurally correct (23/23 design checks); VITE_BASE=/acme-widgets/ build emits correctly prefixed assets and CSV path; postbuild creates byte-identical dist/404.html for SPA fallback; default-base build + e2e remain green. Path correction (private→public, ARCHITECTURE override) honored.

title: GitHub Actions CI workflow (build + vitest + playwright)
description: Per ADR 0001: GitHub Actions free tier. .github/workflows/ci.yml runs install, build, vitest, and playwright on every PR. Must be green before merge.
design:
# CI workflow — design

## File

`.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

## Triggers

- `pull_request` targeting `main`.
- `push` to `main` (so post-merge runs are recorded).
- `workflow_dispatch` (manual reruns).

## Concurrency

```yaml
concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

## Permissions

Top-level `contents: read`. **No `pages: write`** — that lives in the deploy workflow (.11). Principle of least privilege.

## Single `test` job (ubuntu-latest)

Steps in order:
1. `actions/checkout@v4`.
2. `oven-sh/setup-bun@v2` with a **pinned** `bun-version` (do not use `latest` — supply-chain hygiene).
3. `bun install --frozen-lockfile`.
4. `bun run typecheck`.
5. `bun run build`.
6. `bun run test` (Vitest).
7. Cache Playwright browsers: `actions/cache@v4` keyed on the resolved `@playwright/test` version (read from `bun.lockb` via a small `bun pm ls` step, or hardcode the version string and update on bumps).
8. `bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium`.
9. `bun run test:e2e`.
10. On any step failure, upload `playwright-report/` and `test-results/` as artifacts (retention 7 days).

## Branch protection (manual GitHub UI step)

Document in PR description: in repo Settings → Branches, require the `test` job to pass on PRs to `main`. The workflow file alone cannot enforce this.

## Out of scope

- No separate `lint` job (no linter per ADR 0001).
- No matrix across OS/Bun versions — single ubuntu-latest, single Bun version.
- No coverage upload.

## Why one job, not parallel jobs

Project size doesn't justify the install/checkout duplication of multi-job workflows. Revisit only if total CI runtime exceeds ~5 min.
acceptance_criteria: A no-op PR to main runs the test job and lands green. A PR that breaks typecheck, build, Vitest, or Playwright fails the job at the corresponding step with a clear error. On Playwright failure, playwright-report/ and test-results/ are downloadable as workflow artifacts (7-day retention). Concurrency cancels superseded runs on the same ref.
notes:
REVIEW PASSED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30):

Verified at 75640ab. Three of the four canonical acceptance criteria are verifiable from the working tree; the fourth requires a live PR run.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. 'A no-op PR to main runs the test job and lands green' — INHERENTLY POST-MERGE. Strongest possible pre-merge evidence:
   - bun run typecheck: exit 0
   - bun run build: exit 0
   - bun run test (vitest): exit 0 (2 files, 8 tests)
   - bun run test:e2e: exit 0 (3/3 specs in 2.7s)
   The workflow runs the same exact commands; with all gates green locally + a clean lockfile + frozen-lockfile install, the first PR run is overwhelmingly likely to be green.
2. 'PR breaking X fails at corresponding step' — VERIFIED BY STRUCTURE: each gate is its own named step (Typecheck / Build / Vitest / Playwright). GH Actions surfaces the failing step name on failure. Each command independently exits non-zero on failure (verified in .8 / .9 reviews).
3. 'Playwright failure → report + test-results artifacts, 7d retention' — VERIFIED BY YAML: if: failure() + actions/upload-artifact@v4 + retention-days: 7 for both playwright-report/ and test-results/.
4. 'Concurrency cancels superseded runs on same ref' — VERIFIED BY YAML: concurrency.group=ci-${{ github.ref }} + cancel-in-progress: true. Exact GH Actions semantics for this.

Static structural review (23/23 PASS):
- pull_request→main, push→main, workflow_dispatch ✓
- concurrency group + cancel-in-progress: true ✓
- top-level permissions: contents: read; no pages: write anywhere ✓
- ubuntu-latest single 'test' job ✓
- checkout@v4, setup-bun@v2 with bun-version: 1.3.11 (NOT 'latest') ✓
- bun install --frozen-lockfile (and bun.lock is tracked in git) ✓
- typecheck → build → test (vitest) → cache@v4 ms-playwright keyed on PLAYWRIGHT_VERSION env (1.59.1, matches @playwright/test in package.json) → playwright install --with-deps chromium → test:e2e ✓
- failure() upload-artifact@v4 for both playwright-report/ and test-results/ with retention-days: 7 ✓

ARCHITECTURE: confirmed CI canonically runs on the public remote; private repo runs the same workflow as a side effect (acceptable for training).

REVIEWER NOTE: criterion #1 will be observed on the first PR opened against main. If the run is red, file as a new bug — do not reopen .10. Tech-lead's NOTES already acknowledge this iteration model.

MANUAL FOLLOW-UP (cannot be enforced by the workflow file): repo Settings → Branches on the public repo, require the 'test' job on PRs to main. Tech-lead correctly flagged this in NOTES + commit body — surface it in the eventual PR description.

Closing.

title: Set up Playwright with E2E tests for the three routes
description: Per TESTING.md: e2e/ directory at repo root, 'bun run test:e2e' script, headless in CI. Tests: landing on /, browsing /widgets (verify rows from widgets.csv), reading /orders (verify email).
design:
# Playwright E2E — design

## Install

- Dev deps: `@playwright/test`.
- Local: `bunx playwright install chromium`.
- CI: `bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium`.

## Config — `playwright.config.ts`

```ts
testDir: "./e2e",
fullyParallel: true,
forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,
retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,
workers: 1,                              // start strict; raise once stable
reporter: process.env.CI ? [["github"], ["html", { open: "never" }]] : "list",
use: { baseURL: process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL ?? "http://localhost:4173" },
webServer: {
  command: "bun run preview",
  url: "http://localhost:4173",
  reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
  timeout: 120_000,
},
projects: [{ name: "chromium", use: devices["Desktop Chrome"] }],
```

## Why preview, not dev

`vite preview` serves the production build → exercises the same artifact CI deploys → no HMR flakiness.

## Tests (under `e2e/`)

- `e2e/home.spec.ts` — `goto("/")`; assert `getByRole("heading", { level: 1, name: /Acme Widgets/i })` and at least one CTA link to `/widgets`.
- `e2e/widgets.spec.ts` — `goto("/widgets")`; wait for at least one widget card; assert visible text "Classic Brass Widget" (stable row from `widgets.csv`); assert a description containing a comma renders intact (specifically check the "Classic Brass Widget" description with its embedded comma).
- `e2e/orders.spec.ts` — `goto("/orders")`; assert visible text `sales@acmewidgets.com`; assert link `getByRole("link", { name: /sales@acmewidgets.com/ })` has href `mailto:sales@acmewidgets.com`.

## Constraints

- Chromium only — every Playwright spec costs CI minutes (per `TESTING.md` policy). Add Firefox/WebKit only with justification.
- Do NOT mock the network. The live `widgets.csv` (15 rows) is the fixture; specs should be robust to row reordering by querying for stable name strings, not row positions.
- `e2e/` is at the repo root, NOT under `src/`. Vitest excludes it (see .8).

## package.json scripts

- `"test:e2e": "playwright test"`
- `"test:e2e:headed": "playwright test --headed"` (matches `bun run test:e2e -- --headed` in TESTING.md; pick one form and document).
acceptance_criteria: bun run test:e2e runs all three specs against bun run preview and exits 0. Each spec finishes under ~10 seconds locally. Removing the /orders <h1> in source causes orders.spec to fail with a useful error pointing at the missing heading. forbidOnly is enforced in CI (a stray .only() fails the run).

design:
# Vitest — design

## Dev dependencies

- `vitest`
- `@testing-library/react`
- `@testing-library/jest-dom`
- `@testing-library/user-event`
- `jsdom`

(Use `@vitest/ui` only if a maintainer asks for it; out of default scope.)

## Configuration

Put the `test` block inside `vite.config.ts` (single config file — easier for trainees to discover). Required fields:

```ts
test: {
  environment: "jsdom",
  globals: false,                         // require explicit imports
  setupFiles: ["./src/test/setup.ts"],
  css: true,                              // so Tailwind classes don't break renders
  exclude: ["**/node_modules/**", "**/dist/**", "e2e/**"],
}
```

`src/test/setup.ts` imports `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest` and registers `afterEach(cleanup)` from `@testing-library/react`.

## Sample tests (per TESTING.md "co-located")

1. `src/data/widgets.test.ts` — covers the highest-value pure surface in the app:
   - Multi-row CSV including a row with a quoted comma in `description` → description is intact.
   - Empty-but-valid CSV (`type,description,number_in_stock,price,icon,categories\n`) → returns `[]`.
   - Single row → `Widget` fields correctly typed (numbers numeric, categories array).
   - Malformed price (`"abc"`) → either throws or yields `NaN`-handled fallback (pick one and assert).

2. `src/components/Nav.test.tsx` — renders `<Nav />` inside the v7 test router (`createMemoryRouter` + `RouterProvider`); asserts three `NavLink`s present and that clicking "Widgets" updates the location.

## package.json scripts

- `"test": "vitest run"`
- `"test:watch": "vitest"`

`docs/current/TESTING.md` mentions `bun run test --watch`. That maps to passthrough → `vitest --watch`. Either keep both shapes working or update the doc; do NOT diverge silently.

## Out of scope

- Coverage reporting (per ADR 0001).
- Snapshot tests.
- Visual regression.

Container `max-w-2xl mx-auto px-6 py-20`. ## Composition (top to bottom) 1. **Decorative cog stamp** — `` in `text-brass-100`, `w-24 h-24`, centered, with a circular `border border-brass-200` ring around it. `aria-hidden`. No animation. 2. **H1** — `Online orders are coming soon.` — `font-display font-black text-[40px] text-ink text-center mt-6`. 3. **Sub** — `font-sans text-[16px] text-ink-soft text-center mt-3 max-w-prose mx-auto`: `In the meantime, we'll handle your order the old-fashioned way: in writing, by hand, by a real human.` 4. **Mailto card** (`mt-10 rounded-2xl border border-ink/10 bg-paper p-6 shadow-sm`): - Eyebrow: `font-sans font-medium text-[11px] tracking-[0.2em] uppercase text-brass-700`: `EMAIL OUR SALES DESK`. - Email link: `sales@acmewidgets.com` — `font-display font-bold text-[28px] text-brass-700 hover:text-brass-600 underline decoration-2 underline-offset-4 break-all`. - Helper line: `font-sans text-[13px] text-ink-soft mt-3`: `Tell us which widgets, how many, and where to ship them. We reply within one business day.` 5. **Closing line** — `mt-10 text-center font-sans italic text-[13px] text-ink-soft`: `Yes, we know it's 2026. Yes, we still email.` ## Behavior - The email is the **only** interactive element on the page. Don't add a fake form, don't add `mailto:?subject=` prefill (training scope; keep it simple). - Use a real ``, not a button — accessibility tree should call it a link. ## Acceptance - One `

`, no other headings. - The email is selectable, copyable, and clickable. - Renders cleanly at 360px (no overflow on `break-all`). - No console errors; passes a11y contrast check.

title: Build Widgets catalog page (/widgets)
description: Fetch public/data/widgets.csv at runtime, parse client-side, render every widget. Schema: type,description,number_in_stock,price,icon,categories. Per ADR 0001 open question, default to runtime fetch (not build-time import).
design:
# Widgets catalog — design

This is the visual heart of the site. The cog is the brand: every widget renders as a `Cog6ToothIcon` or `Cog8ToothIcon`, and variety comes from **finish, frame, and surrounding visual treatment**, never from changing the silhouette.

## Page layout

- Container: `max-w-6xl mx-auto px-6 py-12`.
- Page header:
  - H1 `The Catalog` — `font-display font-black text-[44px]`.
  - Sub `font-sans text-[16px] text-ink-soft mt-2 max-w-prose`: `Fifteen widgets. One for every purpose, occasion, and excuse.`
- Below the header: a **category filter strip** — pill chips for the union of `categories` values, deduped. `Showing all` is the default chip and is styled as primary. Clicking a chip filters; URL syncs via `?category=birthday` (single value, optional). No multi-select for v1. Counts are shown next to each chip in `text-[11px] text-ink-soft`.
- Grid: `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6 mt-8`.

## Loading & error states

- **Loading:** 6 skeleton cards (same shape as `<WidgetCard />`, replaced with `bg-ink/5 animate-pulse` blocks). Don't show a spinner.
- **Error:** centered card with display-serif `Couldn't load the catalog.` and an inline retry button. Don't blame the user.
- **Empty after filter:** display-serif `No widgets in this category — yet.` plus a "Show everything" link that clears the filter.

## Card chrome (shared by all 15 presets)

`<WidgetCard widget={...} />` — file: `src/components/WidgetCard.tsx`.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│                                  │
│           [HERO REGION]          │  ← preset visuals live ONLY here
│            (180px tall)          │
│                                  │
├──────────────────────────────────┤
│ Type                       $X.XX │  ← display serif title; sans price
│ Description (clamp 2 lines)      │  ← text-[13px] text-ink-soft
│                                  │
│ ● 42 in stock     birthday gift  │  ← stock pill + first 2 categories
└──────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Outer: `rounded-2xl border border-ink/10 bg-paper overflow-hidden transition-shadow hover:shadow-lg focus-within:shadow-lg`.
- Hero: `relative h-[180px] flex items-center justify-center` — preset classes layer on top.
- Body: `p-5 space-y-2`.
- Title: `font-display font-bold text-[20px] text-ink`.
- Price: `font-sans font-semibold text-[16px] text-ink tabular-nums`. Format as `$X.XX` (always 2 decimals).
- Description: `text-[13px] text-ink-soft line-clamp-2`.
- Stock pill: small dot + text. ≥ 50 green, 10–49 amber, < 10 red. **Color is decorative — the number itself carries meaning.** Format: `42 in stock` (low: `Only 9 left`, zero: `Backorder`).
- Categories: first 2 only, `font-sans text-[11px] uppercase tracking-wider text-ink-soft`.

## Cog hover behavior
On card hover (or focus-within), the cog rotates 360° over 1.6s ease-out. Each card stamps a CSS variable so different presets can override (e.g. wedding rotates more slowly). Reduced-motion: no rotation.

## The 15 widget presets — required, exact mapping

The CSV's `icon` field is the lookup key. **Each preset is a different look-and-feel for a hero region containing a heroicons cog.** Use `Cog6ToothIcon` (six teeth) or `Cog8ToothIcon` (eight teeth) per `/24/solid` from `@heroicons/react`. The cog is the constant; the treatment varies.

> Implementation pattern: `src/components/widgetPresets.tsx` exports a map `{ [iconKey]: { cog: 6 | 8, hero: ReactNode | (() => ReactNode), label?: string } }`. `<WidgetCard />` looks up the preset by `widget.icon`. Unknown keys fall back to the **Standard** preset.

| `icon` key              | Cog | Hero treatment |
|-------------------------|----:|----------------|
| `brass-widget.svg`      | 6   | **Brass.** Cog rendered in `text-brass-600` with a faint inner highlight via `drop-shadow-[0_2px_0_rgba(255,255,255,0.4)]`. Background: radial gradient cream→brass-50. A thin engraved line of micro-text reads `ACME · EST. 1952 · BRASS` arcing along the top in 8px display-serif italic. |
| `steel-widget.svg`      | 8   | **Precision steel.** Cog in `text-gunmetal`. Background is a subtle blueprint grid (`bg-blueprint` paper with 1px `text-blueprint-line` 16px grid via repeating-linear-gradient). Faint dimensional callouts ("0.001mm" + caliper tick marks) at corners in 9px monospace. |
| `pocket-widget.svg`     | 6   | **Pocket Mini.** The cog is small (`w-12 h-12`) inside a stitched leather pocket vignette (rounded rect, dashed border `border-dashed border-walnut`). Caption `Travel-sized` floats above in 10px italic. |
| `sparkle-widget.svg`    | 6   | **Birthday Sparkle.** Cog in `text-sparkle-pink`. Confetti dots (CSS-generated colored circles, ~12 of them, randomized positions but seeded) scattered around. Cog rotates faster on hover (1.0s). |
| `heirloom-widget.svg`   | 8   | **Wedding heirloom.** Cog in `text-silver`, set inside an oval velvet-burgundy "box" (`bg-burgundy rounded-[40%] p-8 shadow-inner`). Above: tiny serif text `Acme & Co. · Heirloom Series`. Cog rotates very slowly on hover (3s). |
| `evergreen-widget.svg`  | 8   | **Holiday Evergreen.** Cog in `text-evergreen` with a `gold` outer-stroke ring (`drop-shadow` of gold). Background a deep evergreen-tinted gradient with two faint snowflakes (using `XMarkIcon` rotated, `text-paper/40`) in opposite corners. |
| `industrial-widget.svg` | 8   | **Heavy-Duty Industrial.** Cog in `text-gunmetal` with a chunky bolted plate behind it: 4 small filled circles at the hero corners suggesting bolts. Background `bg-gunmetal/5` with diagonal hatching. Stencil label across the bottom in monospace `RATED · 24/7 · MIL-SPEC`. |
| `walnut-widget.svg`     | 6   | **Artisan Walnut.** Cog rendered in `text-walnut` with a wood-grain background generated from a horizontally-banded SVG (multiple wavy lines in walnut + copper tones). Hand-letter style: tag in lower-right `font-display italic text-[10px]: №   __  / Vermont`. |
| `standard-widget.svg`   | 6   | **Standard / Replacement.** The "default" preset. Cog in `text-ink-soft` on plain `bg-paper` with a single hairline circle. Caption `STANDARD ISSUE` in 10px tracking-wide. This is also the fallback for unknown `icon` keys. |
| `gold-widget.svg`       | 8   | **Anniversary Gold.** Cog in `text-gold` (with subtle `drop-shadow` for sheen) atop a polished marble plinth — render as a small `bg-marble` rounded-base shape with a hairline shadow. Background gradient cream → marble. |
| `lab-widget.svg`        | 8   | **Lab calibration.** Cog in `text-blueprint` on a clinical white `bg-white` background. Calibration certificate banner in lower-right: `bg-paper border border-ink/20 px-2 py-1 text-[9px] font-mono`: `NIST-TRACEABLE · CERT #__`. The cog has a tiny crosshair overlay. |
| `rainbow-widget.svg`    | 6   | **Kids' Rainbow.** Cog filled with a `conic-gradient` masked clip (use `[mask-image]` arbitrary or an SVG gradient `<defs>`). Background `bg-paper` with three hand-drawn-feel arcs in primary colors above the cog. Soft, friendly. Cog rotates with bouncy easing on hover. |
| `graduation-widget.svg` | 8   | **Graduation.** Cog in `text-ink` with a simple square mortarboard shape sitting at the top-left of the cog (CSS-only: a small rotated square + tassel line). Caption `CLASS OF ____` in italic display serif underneath. |
| `bulk-widget.svg`       | 6   | **Workshop bulk-pack.** Render **three** stacked cogs at slight offsets (`-rotate-6`, `0`, `+rotate-6`) suggesting a stack of 25. Background plain kraft paper tone (`bg-parchment`). Stamp graphic in the corner: `× 25` in chunky display serif. |
| `copper-widget.svg`     | 6   | **Vintage copper.** Cog in `text-copper` with an aged-poster vignette: `bg-parchment` background, faded ring border, and a band across the bottom reading `THE 1952 ACME CATALOG · NO. 0007` in 9px display-serif uppercase. |

### Constraints
- Every preset's hero is **pure CSS + heroicons cog + small inline SVG primitives**. No raster art, no per-widget asset files. The CSV's `icon` field is a *key*, not a path to fetch.
- Every preset must work at the card's 180px hero height; do not let preset chrome force the card taller.
- Keep total preset CSS budget light; use Tailwind utilities first, plugin only if a preset truly needs it.
- All preset chrome is `aria-hidden`. The card's accessible label is the widget `type`.

## Card interaction
- Whole card is **not** a link (per IA: no detail page). Don't make it look clickable. Hover affordance is shadow + cog rotation only.
- Categories shown on the card are not interactive in v1 (no click-to-filter from the card). The filter strip up top owns filtering.

## Data layer

- Loader (or hook) lives at `src/lib/widgets.ts`. Single fetch of `${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}data/widgets.csv` (using BASE_URL is required so it works under the Pages base path from aw-l0r.11). Parse with `papaparse` (mainstream, small).
- Type:
  ```ts
  type Widget = {
    type: string;
    description: string;
    numberInStock: number;
    price: number;
    icon: string;          // preset key
    categories: string[];  // split on ';'
  };
  ```
- Coerce `number_in_stock` to int, `price` to float, split `categories` on `;`. Reject rows where `type` or `icon` is empty (don't crash; log + skip).
- Cache the parsed list in module scope so `/widgets` and the home featured trio share one fetch.

## Acceptance
- All 15 CSV rows render with the correct preset hero per the table above; no missing-preset warnings in console.
- Filter strip filters the grid; chip count badges match `<filtered>/<total>`.
- Loading state shows skeleton cards (no layout shift when data arrives).
- Reduced-motion disables every cog rotation.
- Lighthouse a11y ≥ 95 on the page; no contrast warnings; cards are reachable by keyboard tab order in DOM order.
- Page works under both `/` (dev) and `/acme-widgets-private/` (Pages) base paths.
acceptance_criteria: Route loader fetches widgets.csv once on navigation; component renders all 15 rows. The quoted-comma description on "Classic Brass Widget" renders intact (no truncation at the comma). Categories render as separated chips (semicolon split). Network or parse error triggers the route errorElement with a readable message. Direct deep-link to /widgets (full reload) works under both base paths (/ and /acme-widgets-private/). # Home — design

## Above the fold (no scroll on 1024×768)

Layout: `max-w-6xl mx-auto px-6 py-20`, content left-aligned, decorative motif absolute-right.

- **Eyebrow:** `ACME WIDGETS · EST. 1952` — `font-sans font-medium text-[12px] tracking-[0.2em] text-brass-700`.
- **H1:** `Widgets for all purposes and occasions.` — `font-display font-black text-ink leading-[1.05]`, size `clamp(40px, 6vw, 72px)`. This is the only `<h1>` on the page.
- **Sub:** `A widget for every wonder. Browse the catalog or get in touch — we're old-fashioned that way.` — `font-sans text-[18px] text-ink-soft max-w-prose mt-6`.
- **CTA row** (`mt-8 flex gap-3`):
  - Primary: `<Link to="/widgets">Browse the catalog</Link>` — `bg-brass-600 hover:bg-brass-700 text-paper font-sans font-semibold text-[16px] px-6 py-3 rounded-full shadow-md transition`.
  - Secondary: `<Link to="/orders">How to order</Link>` — `border-2 border-brass-600 text-ink font-sans font-semibold text-[16px] px-6 py-3 rounded-full hover:bg-brass-50 transition`.

### Hero motif
An oversized `<Cog8ToothIcon />` from `/24/outline`, stroke `text-brass-100`, ~520px square, positioned `absolute -right-24 top-12 -z-0` behind the text column. Slow rotation: `animation: spin 60s linear infinite`. `aria-hidden`. Reduced-motion → static (handled globally).

## Below the fold

Section, `mt-24`:
- **Heading:** `Featured this season` — `font-display font-bold text-[28px]`.
- **Sub-line:** `font-sans text-[14px] text-ink-soft mt-1`: `Three widgets, three reasons.`
- **Trio:** a 3-column grid (`grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 mt-8`) reusing `<WidgetCard />` from aw-l0r.6.
- **Selection rule (deterministic):** the **first** widget in `widgets.csv` whose `categories` field contains `everyday`, the first `occasion`, the first `business`. If any of the three is missing, hide that slot. If the catalog fetch errors or returns empty, hide the entire section. Do **not** render error UI on the home page.

Closing line, `mt-20 text-center`:
- `Hand-curated since 1952. Ships from Vermont, the workshop floor, and the calibration lab.` — `font-sans italic text-[14px] text-ink-soft`.

## Data
The featured trio uses the same data hook/loader as `/widgets` — implement once in aw-l0r.6 and import here. Don't duplicate the fetch.

## Implementation
- File: `src/pages/Home.tsx`.
- CTAs are `<Link>` from React Router styled as buttons (preserve link semantics, not `<button>`).
- The 60s rotation animation: `animate-[spin_60s_linear_infinite]`.

## Acceptance
- Hero text and CTAs visible without scroll on a 1024×768 viewport.
- Featured row shows three distinct widgets matching the selection rule against the current CSV.
- Reduced-motion: hero motif is static.
- Single `<h1>`; `<h2>` for "Featured this season".

title: Build top-level navigation component

design:
# Top nav — design

## Layout
Full-width sticky bar at top, height **72px**. Background: `bg-paper/80 backdrop-blur` over the page paper. Hairline 1px bottom border `border-ink/10`. `z-40`.

## Left side — logo + wordmark
- **Logo:** `<Cog6ToothIcon />` (heroicons `/24/solid`), `w-8 h-8`, `text-brass-600`. Wrapped in a `group` with the wordmark.
- **Hover behavior:** the cog rotates **60°** (one tooth-notch) on hover via `group-hover:rotate-[60deg] transition-transform duration-[400ms] ease-out`. `prefers-reduced-motion` removes the rotation (handled globally — see aw-l0r.2).
- **Wordmark:** "Acme Widgets" in `font-display font-bold text-[22px] text-ink`, with a tiny "Est. 1952" superscript in `font-sans text-[11px] text-ink-soft`. The 1952 superscript is the only place this date appears in chrome — it pays off the Vintage Copper Widget's heritage line.

## Right side — three NavLinks
- Use React Router `<NavLink>` for each: Home (`/`), Widgets (`/widgets`), Orders (`/orders`).
- Order: Home, Widgets, Orders (left-to-right).
- Inactive: `font-sans font-medium text-[15px] text-ink-soft hover:text-ink transition-colors`.
- Active (`isActive`): `text-ink` with a 2px `border-b-2 border-brass-600` underline.
- Spacing between links: `gap-8`.

## Mobile (<640px)
Same single-row layout — only 3 links, no hamburger needed. Wordmark drops to "Acme Widgets" without the "Est. 1952" superscript; links shrink to 13px. Verify nothing wraps below 360px.

## Accessibility
- `<nav aria-label="Primary">` wraps the link group.
- **Skip-to-content link** is the first focusable element on the page (renders inside the layout, before the nav). Visually hidden until focus, then anchors to `#main`. The layout from aw-l0r.3 should already wrap content in `<main id="main">`.
- All interactive elements get the brass focus ring (default from base styles).

## Implementation notes
- File: `src/components/TopNav.tsx`. No client state — purely presentational.
- Rendered by the layout route from aw-l0r.3 (replace the placeholder `<header>` introduced there).
- Heroicons import: `import { Cog6ToothIcon } from '@heroicons/react/24/solid'`. Add `@heroicons/react` to dependencies.

## Acceptance
- Active NavLink has a visible brass underline and is the only one with `text-ink`.
- Logo cog rotates on hover (and does NOT rotate when OS reduce-motion is on).
- Tabbing into the page surfaces the skip link first; activating it jumps focus to `#main`.
- Renders cleanly at 360px / 768px / 1280px.
acceptance_criteria: Nav appears on every page (assert in Vitest by rendering RootLayout under any route). Clicking each NavLink updates the URL without a full page reload. The active link exposes aria-current="page" automatically via NavLink. The skip link is reachable on first Tab from URL bar focus and jumps to <main id="main">.
notes:
REVIEW PASSED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30): Verified at 006398d via Playwright on bun run dev + typecheck/build: Acceptance criteria: 1. Nav appears on every page — snapshot on / shows banner + nav[aria-label='Primary'] + 3 links (Home/Widgets/Orders); /orders snapshot also confirms navPresent=true, skipPresent=true, mainHasId=true, mainTabIndex=-1. Vitest assertion deferred — covered by .8 when test runner lands; behavior fully verified end-to-end now. 2. NavLink clicks SPA-update URL (no full reload) — stashed window.__navMarker on /, clicked Widgets then Orders; marker survived both navs, URL changed correctly each time. 3. aria-current='page' moves automatically — Home active on /, Widgets active on /widgets, Orders active on /orders (NavLink end={true} on index handles trailing-slash correctly). 4. Skip link first Tab → main jump — fresh load on /widgets, blurred focus, single Tab → activeElement is Skip to content; Enter → activeElement is
(tabIndex=-1 makes the focus jump real, not just an anchor scroll). Hash becomes #main as expected. Design + code: - Sticky header h-[72px], bg-paper/80 + backdrop-blur, ink/10 hairline ✓ - Cog rotation: group-hover:rotate-[60deg] + duration-400 (zeroed by aw-l0r.2's prefers-reduced-motion global) ✓ - nav aria-label='Primary' wraps the link group ✓ - Skip link is the FIRST focusable element on the page ✓; sr-only until focus, then visible - 44 lines of TopNav.tsx, presentational, no client state ✓ - Heroicons solid Cog6ToothIcon, w-8 h-8 text-brass-600 ✓; aria-hidden on the icon - routes.tsx replaces placeholder header with TopNav, adds skip-to-content + tabIndex=-1 on main ✓ Responsive (verified 360 / 768 / 1280): - 360: header height 72, no horizontal overflow (bodyScrollWidth=docClientWidth=360), all 3 links on a single visual row, 'Est. 1952' superscript hidden, wordmark at 17px - 768: no overflow, sup visible, wordmark 22px - 1280: no overflow, sup visible, wordmark 22px Console: 0 errors except the pre-existing /favicon.ico 404 from .1 (out of scope). Re tech-lead's two questions: - 17px sm:22px wordmark + gap-4 sm:gap-8 mobile compromise: APPROVED. Design said 'no hamburger needed' and 'nothing wraps below 360px' — this is the cleanest path that honors both. Hiding the wordmark would lose brand on mobile. - Defer vitest assertion until .8 lands: APPROVED. .4 doesn't depend on .8; .8 will own the layout test as part of its own scope. Adding a one-off vitest config now would be wasted work undone by .8. Closing.

title: Set up React Router v7 data router with routes.tsx
description: Per docs/current/INFORMATION_ARCHITECTURE.md, declare all routes (/, /widgets, /orders) in a single src/routes.tsx using React Router v7 data router. Wire it into main.tsx via RouterProvider. Layout route hosts the top nav.
design:
# React Router v7 — design

## Mode

**Data router only.** Use `createBrowserRouter` + `<RouterProvider router={router} />`. NOT framework mode (no Remix-style file conventions). NOT `<BrowserRouter>` JSX (that is the legacy mode).

## Single source of truth

`src/routes.tsx` exports `router`. Trainees see the entire IA in one file (per `INFORMATION_ARCHITECTURE.md`).

## Layout pattern

```
path: "/" → <RootLayout />     # renders <Nav /> (.4) + <main id="main"><Outlet /></main>
  index → Home (.5)
  widgets → Widgets (.6)       # loader added by .6
  orders → Orders (.7)
```

## basename — REQUIRED

```ts
const router = createBrowserRouter(routes, { basename: import.meta.env.BASE_URL });
```

This is what makes the app work both at `/` in dev and at `/acme-widgets-private/` on Pages (.11). **Do not hardcode `/`.**

## Errors

Every route gets an `errorElement`. The root layout's `errorElement` covers unmatched paths. Implementation: a small `<RouteError />` reading `useRouteError()`. In dev, surface the error message; in prod, generic copy.

## Types

`const routes: RouteObject[] = [...]` — annotate for type safety.

## main.tsx update

Replace any placeholder render with `<RouterProvider router={router} />` inside `<StrictMode>`.

## Out of scope for .3

- Loaders for `/widgets` — added by .6.
- The `<Nav />` component — added by .4. Until .4 lands, the layout can render `<header>placeholder</header>` so unblocked work can start.
acceptance_criteria: All three paths (/, /widgets, /orders) render placeholders without console errors. Direct navigation to /widgets (page refresh in dev) works. An unknown path (e.g. /nope) renders the errorElement, not a blank screen. The layout wrapper renders <Outlet /> consistently across routes.
notes:
REVIEW PASSED (acme-widgets/reviewer, 2026-04-30):

Verified at 3e1c340:
- bun run typecheck: exit 0
- bun run build: exit 0; emits dist/assets/index-9rVaoor-.js + index-XU_QDND9.css
- Playwright on bun run dev (http://localhost:5173):
  - / → header 'nav goes here' + main + h1 'Acme Widgets' + 'Home placeholder.'; 0 console errors (only pre-existing /favicon.ico 404, out of scope per .1)
  - /widgets (direct nav, SPA fallback) → same banner + main with 'Widgets' + 'Catalog placeholder.'; 0 console errors
  - /orders → same banner + main with 'Orders' + 'Orders placeholder.'; 0 console errors
  - /nope → root errorElement renders '404 Not Found' + 'We couldn't find that page.' (NOT blank). The two 'No routes matched location' lines are React Router dev WARNINGS, not errors — expected behavior when unmatched paths fall through to the boundary.

Layout: <Outlet/> renders consistently across the three real routes (banner + main + page-specific content). For /nope, the root-level errorElement replaces the layout entirely — matches the design note 'root layout's errorElement covers unmatched paths'.

Code review: createBrowserRouter ✓; basename: import.meta.env.BASE_URL ✓ (unblocks .11 Pages deploy); src/routes.tsx is the single source of truth ✓; typed RouteObject[] ✓; per-leaf errorElement + root errorElement ✓; useRouteError + isRouteErrorResponse with dev-only detail leak ✓; main.tsx uses RouterProvider inside StrictMode ✓; App.tsx deleted ✓; placeholder header for nav respects bead NOTES (unblocks .4/.5/.6/.7 in parallel) ✓; Orders uses max-w-2xl per design ✓.

Closing.

# Tailwind — design tokens & fonts

## tailwind.config.ts — extend theme

```ts
colors: {
  paper:        '#FBF7EF',  // page bg, warm cream
  ink:          '#1B1A17',  // body text
  'ink-soft':   '#3F3B33',  // secondary text
  brass: {
    50:  '#FBF1D8',
    100: '#F5DDA0',
    400: '#D4A53A',
    600: '#A87B1F',  // primary
    700: '#7C5715',
  },
  copper:       '#B36A3D',
  walnut:       '#5A3B22',
  evergreen:    '#1F4D3A',
  gunmetal:     '#2E343B',
  silver:       '#C7CCD1',
  gold:         '#D4AF37',
  burgundy:     '#5B1A1F',
  'sparkle-pink':'#FF4FA3',
  marble:       '#EFEDE6',
  parchment:    '#EFE2C2',
  blueprint:    '#0E2A47',
  'blueprint-line':'#7AA8D8',
},
fontFamily: {
  display: ['"Fraunces"', 'Georgia', 'serif'],
  sans:    ['Inter', 'system-ui', 'sans-serif'],
},
borderRadius: { '2xl': '1rem' },
```

Rainbow accent uses `bg-[conic-gradient(...)]` ad-hoc; no token needed.

## Fonts — Google Fonts via index.html

Self-hosting is overkill for a training repo. Add `<link rel="preconnect">` + Google Fonts CSS link with `display=swap`:
- **Fraunces** weights 700, 900 (regular + italic)
- **Inter** weights 400, 500, 600

## Base styles (src/index.css after `@tailwind` directives)

```css
html { @apply bg-paper text-ink font-sans antialiased; }
body { @apply min-h-screen; background-image: var(--paper-texture); }
h1, h2, h3 { @apply font-display; }

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *, *::before, *::after {
    animation-duration: 0ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0ms !important;
  }
}
```

## Paper texture utility

Subtle SVG noise as a CSS data-URL, set on `body` at ~0.04 opacity. Implement as a Tailwind plugin utility `bg-paper-texture` (or a `--paper-texture` CSS variable on `:root`) so pages don't repeat the URL inline. Either works; pick one and stick to it.

## Container default

`max-w-6xl mx-auto px-6` is the default page container. Pages override as needed (Orders is `max-w-2xl`).

## Acceptance
- A throwaway test page using `bg-paper text-ink font-display text-4xl` and `bg-brass-600` renders with the right vibe in `bun run dev`.
- Toggling OS-level "Reduce motion" disables transitions globally.
- Lighthouse contrast check passes on default text colors.
acceptance_criteria: A test class such as text-3xl font-bold text-blue-600 renders styled in bun run dev. bun run build emits a minified CSS asset under dist/. No console warnings about unknown @apply directives or missing PostCSS plugins.

title: Bootstrap Bun + Vite + React + TypeScript project
description: Create package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, index.html, src/main.tsx, src/App.tsx so 'bun install' and 'bun run dev' produce a working React app per ADR 0001. No routing or styling yet — that's separate tasks.
design:
# Bootstrap — design

## Versions (pin in package.json)

- Bun: `>=1.2` in `engines`. Document min in README.
- React: `^19` (RR v7 supports 18+; default to 19 by 2026-04).
- TypeScript: `^5.7+`.
- Vite: `^6+`. Use `@vitejs/plugin-react`.

## tsconfig.json (strict)

- `"strict": true`, `"noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true`, `"noImplicitOverride": true`.
- `"moduleResolution": "bundler"`, `"module": "ESNext"`, `"target": "ES2022"`.
- `"jsx": "react-jsx"`, `"isolatedModules": true`, `"verbatimModuleSyntax": true`.
- `"types": ["vite/client"]` so `import.meta.env` is typed.
- `"include": ["src", "vite.config.ts"]`. Separate `tsconfig.node.json` for tooling.

## Project layout

```
/index.html
/src/main.tsx        # ReactDOM.createRoot, StrictMode wrap
/src/App.tsx         # placeholder, replaced by RouterProvider in .3
/vite.config.ts      # react plugin; base wired in .11
/package.json        # scripts: dev, build, preview, typecheck
/tsconfig.json
/tsconfig.node.json
```

## package.json scripts

- `"dev": "vite"`
- `"build": "tsc -b && vite build"` (typecheck on build)
- `"preview": "vite preview"`
- `"typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"`
- Test scripts come in .8/.9; do not stub them here.

## Conventions

- No path aliases — keep imports relative; this is a teaching repo.
- No ESLint/Prettier per ADR 0001 scope. If added later, file a new ADR.
- StrictMode wraps the root in `main.tsx`.
- Dev server defaults (port 5173, host localhost). Do not customize.
acceptance_criteria: bun install succeeds on a clean clone with no peer-dep warnings. bun run dev starts Vite (default port 5173) and renders a placeholder page. bun run build produces dist/ with index.html plus hashed JS. bun run preview serves the built output identically. bun run typecheck exits 0.

- bun install: clean, 69 pkgs, no peer-dep warnings

- bun run dev: serves placeholder page (default port 5173; environmentally fell back to 5174 because another local project held 5173 — not a defect; default config is correct)
- bun run preview: serves built output at 4173, HTTP 200

design:
# Acme Widgets — Design System

## Brand voice
Confident, warm, slightly retro, gently whimsical. Smithsonian gift shop × 1952 Acme catalog. Borrow the cadence of "a thneed is something that everyone needs": short, declarative, faintly silly. No corporate filler. The Vintage Copper Widget in the catalog ties to the year 1952 — that heritage is the brand's anchor.

## Tagline
- Primary: **"Widgets for all purposes and occasions."**
- Supporting: **"A widget for every wonder."**

## Visual language
- **The cog is the brand.** Every widget on the site renders as a heroicons `Cog6ToothIcon` or `Cog8ToothIcon` — never a different shape. Variety comes from finish, framing, and surrounding visual treatment, not from the silhouette. Consistency in form, riot of variety in presentation. (See aw-l0r.6 for the 15-preset treatment system.)
- **Palette base:** paper-cream background, ink-black text, brass primary. Per-widget accents are scoped to the card hero region; page chrome stays neutral.
- **Type pairing:** chunky display serif for headings (Fraunces), workhorse sans for body (Inter). Display serif gives the catalog its old-Acme personality; sans keeps long copy readable.
- **Surface feel:** generous whitespace, hairline borders, soft shadows on hover/active only, subtle paper texture on backgrounds. No flat-design starkness. 8px grid.
- **Decorative motif:** a faint, oversized cog may appear in page backgrounds (rotating very slowly is welcome). Used at most once per page.

## Accessibility (binding for all UI tasks)
- All text ≥ WCAG AA contrast.
- Focus rings visible (2px brass-400 offset 2px).
- Color is never the sole carrier of meaning — stock pills carry text, not just color.
- `prefers-reduced-motion` disables every rotation, including the logo cog hover, the home hero motif, and the cog-on-card hover.
- Decorative SVGs are `aria-hidden`.

## Personas → design (where they show up)
Per `docs/current/PERSONAS.md`, no per-persona UI. Personas show up in **copy and catalog presentation**:
- Kids → Pocket Mini, Rainbow: bright, soft, hand-drawn energy.
- Industrial/business → Steel, Industrial, Lab: precise, gridded, stamped.
- Occasion → Birthday, Wedding, Anniversary, Graduation, Holiday: each gets a frame appropriate to the moment.
- Heirloom/artisanal → Brass, Walnut, Copper: heritage cues (engraving, wood-grain, vintage poster).

## Per-task design specs (binding)
- aw-l0r.2 — design tokens, fonts, paper texture utility
- aw-l0r.4 — top nav (sticky, animated cog logo, NavLink active state)
- aw-l0r.5 — home (hero, featured trio, motif)
- aw-l0r.6 — widgets catalog (card chrome, states, the 15-preset treatment system)
- aw-l0r.7 — orders (mailto card, copy voice)

Implementers: treat the per-task `DESIGN` blocks as binding. Deviate only with a comment in the task and a quick check-in.

comments.text:
Plan ready for agent peer review and human review.

12 child tasks (aw-l0r.1 through aw-l0r.12) with dependency graph:

  .1 bootstrap (Bun+Vite+React+TS)
   |- .2 Tailwind
   |- .3 React Router v7 / routes.tsx
   |   |- .4 Top nav  (also needs .2)
   |   |- .5 Home /   (also needs .2)
   |   |- .6 /widgets (also needs .2; consumes public/data/widgets.csv)
   |   |- .7 /orders  (also needs .2)
   |- .8 Vitest + sample tests
   |- .11 GitHub Pages deploy
  .9  Playwright E2E   (needs .5, .6, .7)
  .10 CI workflow      (needs .8, .9)
  .12 Move ADR + sync docs/current  (final; needs .10, .11)

Sources reviewed:
- docs/future/0001.ADR.TECH_STACK.md (stack, hosting, testing, default branch, runtime CSV fetch)
- docs/current/INFORMATION_ARCHITECTURE.md (3 routes, single nav, single routes.tsx)
- docs/current/PERSONAS.md (read-only catalog, no auth, no per-persona UI)
- docs/current/TESTING.md (Vitest co-located, Playwright in e2e/)
- docs/current/DOCUMENTATION.md (ADR lifecycle: future/ -> decision-records/ on impl branch, sync docs/current/)

Open questions for reviewers:
1. ADR is still 'Proposed' - should aw-l0r.12 also flip status to 'Accepted' when moving to decision-records/?
2. Is widgets.csv (already populated, 15 rows) sufficient for .6 and .9, or add an empty-state fixture?
3. Vite base path for GitHub project pages (.11) - deploy at user-pages root vs /acme-widgets-private/?

Holding on implementation pending review.

title: TopNav overflows ~10px at 360px viewport when vertical scrollbar is present
description: At a 360px-wide window with content tall enough to force a vertical scrollbar, the desktop scrollbar (~15px) reduces effective viewport width to ~345px and the TopNav extends to right=355px (Orders link is the rightmost overflowing element). aw-l0r.4 verified TopNav at 360×800 with no scrollbar, so this regression only manifests when scrollbar steals width. Not a blocker for any specific page; affects every page when content is taller than the short-window. Suggest reducing nav gap, shrinking cog, or hiding the wordmark below 380px. Reproduce: open /orders at 360×700 (any short viewport that triggers vertical scrollbar). Found while reviewing aw-l0r.7.
